chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُعْطِي عَنْ زَكَاتِهِ الْعِوَضَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 42 – The man who gives in lieu of his Zakāt

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/106)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/106)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ مُوسَى ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُعْطِي عَنْ زَكَاتِهِ مِنَ الدَّرَاهِمِ دَنَانِيرَ وَ عَنِ الدَّنَانِيرِ دَرَاهِمَ بِالْقِيمَةِ أَ يَحِلُّ ذَلِكَ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Al Amraky Bin Ali, from Ali Bin Ja’far who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan Musaasws about the man who gives from his Zakāt of Dirhams, Dinars, and from his Zakāt of Dinars, Dirhams, by the price. Is that Permissible for him?’ Heasws said: ‘There is no problem with it’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from al-‘Amrakiy ibn Ali from ibn Ja‘far who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who pays Zakat due on dirham with dinar and Zakat which is due on dinar with dirham. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful.’”
